revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fujiwara, Kisshi}} [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia15]] [[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia15]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] [[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia3]] [[en:Saionji Kisshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fujiwara, Kisshi}} [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Saionji Kisshi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fujiwara, Kisshi}} [[Category:Fujiwara {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buqqhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess {{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fujiwara, Kisshi}} [[Category:Fujiwara She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess {{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages and categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[sn:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]]

Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[sn:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[sn:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Infobox person|birth_date=1225|death_date={{death date|1292|10|20}}|birth_place={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|qid=Q349259}}|children={{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|qid=Q349246}}; {{ill|Emperor Kameyama|qid=Q360972}}; {{ill|Sōshi-naishinnō|qid=Q11607096}}|father={{ill|Saionji Saneuji|qid=Q3464638}}|mother={{ill|Sadako Sijō|qid=Q11420018}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Kimiko|qid=Q5507518}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|qid=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺姞子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q5507526]] Wikidata name: Fujiwara no Kitsushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[sn:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

[[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1225|death_date={{death date|1292|10|20}}|birth_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259}}|children={{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246}}; {{ill|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972}}; {{ill|Sōshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11607096}}|father={{ill|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638}}|mother={{ill|Sadako Sijō|WD=Q11420018}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Kimiko|WD=Q5507518}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺姞子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q5507526]] Wikidata name: Fujiwara no Kitsushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}}

She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}}

She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1225|death_date={{death date|1292|10|20}}|birth_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259}}|children={{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246}}; {{ill|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972}}; {{ill|Sōshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11607096}}|father={{ill|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638}}|mother={{ill|Sadako Sijō|WD=Q11420018}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Kimiko|WD=Q5507518}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺姞子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5507526]] Wikidata name: Fujiwara no Kitsushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1225|death_date={{death date|1292|10|20}}|birth_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259}}|children={{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246}}; {{ill|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972}}; {{ill|Sōshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11607096}}|father={{ill|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638}}|mother={{ill|Sadako Sijō|WD=Q11420018}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Kimiko|WD=Q5507518}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺姞子}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5507526]] Wikidata name: Fujiwara no Kitsushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|[[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]] *Princess Osako *[[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]] *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Saga|Emperor Go-Saga]]. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Saneuji]] and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Go-Fukakusa|Emperor Go-Fukakusa]]) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ([[:en:Emperor Kameyama|Emperor Kameyama]]) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Rishi|Princess Rishi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Teruko|Princess Teruko]] | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ({{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|en|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246|lt=Emperor Go-Fukakusa}}) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ({{ill|Emperor Kameyama|en|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972|lt=Emperor Kameyama}}) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Rishi|en|Princess Rishi|WD=Q7244792|lt=Princess Rishi}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Princess Teruko|en|Princess Teruko|WD=Q7244849|lt=Princess Teruko}} | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]][[en:Saionji Kisshi]][[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]][[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[ja:西園寺姞子]][[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]][[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]][[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1225|death_date={{death date|1292|10|20}}|birth_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|death_place={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259}}|children={{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246}}; {{ill|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972}}; {{ill|Sōshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11607096}}|father={{ill|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638}}|mother={{ill|Sadako Sijō|WD=Q11420018}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Kimiko|WD=Q5507518}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺姞子}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kishi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|藤原姞子|date=November 2011}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Saionji Kisshi
{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|{{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|en|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259|lt=Emperor Go-Saga}}|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *{{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|en|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246|lt=Emperor Go-Fukakusa}} *Princess Osako *{{ill|Emperor Kameyama|en|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972|lt=Emperor Kameyama}} *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = {{ill|Saionji Saneuji|en|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638|lt=Saionji Saneuji}} | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|en|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259|lt=Emperor Go-Saga}}. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of {{ill|Saionji Saneuji|en|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638|lt=Saionji Saneuji}} and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name}} '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ({{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|en|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246|lt=Emperor Go-Fukakusa}}) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ({{ill|Emperor Kameyama|en|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972|lt=Emperor Kameyama}}) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Rishi|en|Princess Rishi|WD=Q7244792|lt=Princess Rishi}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Princess Teruko|en|Princess Teruko|WD=Q7244849|lt=Princess Teruko}} | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

{{lang|ja|西園寺姞子}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 5 September 1242 – 10 July 1248 | spouse = {{marriage|{{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|en|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259|lt=Emperor Go-Saga|1=Emperor Go-Saga}}|1242|1272|end=died}} | issue = {{plainlist| *{{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|en|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246|lt=Emperor Go-Fukakusa|1=Emperor Go-Fukakusa}} *Princess Osako *{{ill|Emperor Kameyama|en|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972|lt=Emperor Kameyama|1=Emperor Kameyama}} *Prince Masataka *Prince Sadayoshi}} | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = {{ill|Saionji Saneuji|en|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638|lt=Saionji Saneuji|1=Saionji Saneuji}} | mother = Shijō Sadako | birth_date = 1225 | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1292|10|20|1225|df=y}} | death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) | date of burial = | place of burial = }} '''Saionji Kisshi''' (西園寺 姞子; 1225 – 20 October 1292) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|en|Emperor Go-Saga|WD=Q349259|lt=Emperor Go-Saga|1=Emperor Go-Saga}}. She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of {{ill|Saionji Saneuji|en|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638|lt=Saionji Saneuji|1=Saionji Saneuji}} and Shijō Sadako. Upon Emperor Go-Saga's passing, she was ordained as a Buddhist nun and received the {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|WD=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} '''Henchikaku''' (遍智覚).{{cite web |title=大宮院 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E9%99%A2-39596 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |access-date=2019-10-12 |language=ja}} == Family == Issue: **Fourth son: Imperial Prince Hisahito (久仁親王) ({{ill|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|en|Emperor Go-Fukakusa|WD=Q349246|lt=Emperor Go-Fukakusa|1=Emperor Go-Fukakusa}}) **First daughter: Imperial Princess Osako (綜子内親王)Emergence of Japanese Kingship, p5 **Seventh son: Imperial Prince Tsunehito (恒仁親王) ({{ill|Emperor Kameyama|en|Emperor Kameyama|WD=Q360972|lt=Emperor Kameyama|1=Emperor Kameyama}}) **Eleventh son: Imperial Prince Masataka (雅尊親王) **Thirteenth son: Imperial Prince Sadayoshi (貞良親王) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Rishi|en|Princess Rishi|WD=Q7244792|lt=Princess Rishi|1=Princess Rishi}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | after={{ill|Princess Teruko|en|Princess Teruko|WD=Q7244849|lt=Princess Teruko|1=Princess Teruko}} | years=1242–1248}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kisshi]] [[arz:فوجيوارا نو كيتسوشى]] [[fa:سایئونجی کیتسوشی]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[id:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[ja:西園寺姞子]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Kitsushi]] [[th:ฟุจิวะระ โนะ คิสึชิ]] [[zh:西園寺姞子]] {{wikidata link|Q5507526}} [[Category:1225 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

She was also known as '''Ōmiya-in''' (大宮院), her imperial title (''ingō''), and as '''Fujiwara no Kisshi''' (藤原 姞子), by her original clan name (''honsei''). == Biography == She was the first daughter of {{ill|Saionji Saneuji|en|Saionji Saneuji|WD=Q3464638|lt=Saionji Saneuji|1=Saionji Saneuji}} and Shijō Sadako.
